title: Korean Conglomerate's AI Push Raises Questions in Seoul
body: Hanseong Group's move into European artificial intelligence has drawn scrutiny from analysts in Seoul, with some questioning whether the conglomerate's logistics and retail divisions genuinely need AI analytics capabilities or whether the deal reflects pressure from the founding family to pursue technology acquisitions. The group, controlled by the Cho family, has made several overseas deals in recent years with mixed results. Ji-Yeon Kwon, the CFO who has been publicly credited with structuring the Vantara arrangement, is considered a reformer within the company but has limited experience with software licensing. One analyst at Mirae Asset called the five-year licensing deal "a reasonable foot in the door" while others were more skeptical. A minority shareholder group filed a request this week asking Hanseong's board to disclose the full terms of the Vantara agreement, citing concerns about undisclosed commitments. Hanseong said the terms are commercially sensitive.
